business_rules.rule: When no command-line arguments are supplied the application must produce a greeting using the default recipient 'World'
The greeting output must be written to standard output (stdout) terminated with a system newline
The GreetingService dependency injected into GreetingController must not be null; construction must fail immediately if null is supplied
When args is null the controller must treat it as an absent recipient and delegate null to the service
When args is empty (zero-length array) the controller must treat it as an absent recipient and delegate null to the service
Only the first command-line argument (index 0) is considered the recipient; all subsequent arguments are discarded
The GreetingRepository dependency injected into GreetingService must not be null; construction must fail immediately if null is supplied
If the requested recipient is null the service must substitute the repository-supplied default recipient
If the requested recipient is blank (contains only whitespace characters) the service must substitute the repository-supplied default recipient
A non-null non-blank requested recipient must have its leading and trailing whitespace trimmed before being used in the greeting
The greeting message is formed by inserting the resolved recipient into the repository-provided template string
The greeting template is 'Hello %s' where %s is replaced by the resolved recipient name
The greeting template must contain exactly one positional string placeholder so that exactly one recipient name is embedded in the output

workflows.name: Default Greeting Workflow
workflows.description: Full execution path when the application is invoked without command-line arguments

steps.action: Instantiate GreetingRepository, GreetingService, and GreetingController via constructor injection
Call controller.greet(args) with an empty String array
Detect empty args array; pass null to greetingService.createGreeting(null)
Detect null requestedRecipient; retrieve default recipient 'World' from repository
Retrieve greeting template 'Hello %s' from repository; format with 'World' to produce 'Hello World'
Print 'Hello World' to stdout with trailing newline

workflows.name: Named Recipient Greeting Workflow
workflows.description: Full execution path when the application is invoked with a named recipient as the first CLI argument

steps.action: Instantiate GreetingRepository, GreetingService, and GreetingController via constructor injection
Call controller.greet(args) with a non-empty String array
Extract args[0] as the recipient; call greetingService.createGreeting(args[0])
Detect non-null non-blank recipient; trim whitespace to get clean recipient name
Retrieve greeting template 'Hello %s'; format with trimmed recipient to produce 'Hello <name>'
Print 'Hello <name>' to stdout with trailing newline

workflows.name: Blank Recipient Fallback Workflow
workflows.description: Execution path when a blank (whitespace-only) recipient string is provided

steps.action: Invoke GreetingService.createGreeting with a whitespace-only string
Detect blank recipient; retrieve default recipient 'World' from repository
Retrieve greeting template; format with 'World' to produce 'Hello World'

edge_cases.case: Null args array passed to controller.greet
edge_cases.handling: Treated identically to empty array; null is forwarded to service triggering default-recipient fallback
isBlank() check in GreetingService detects it and falls back to default recipient 'World'
Only args[0] is used; args[1..n] are silently ignored
Objects.requireNonNull throws NullPointerException with message 'greetingService must not be null'
Objects.requireNonNull throws NullPointerException with message 'greetingRepository must not be null'
